name: Kaleida Health
description_short: Kaleida Health FCU is a member-owned federal credit union serving healthcare professionals in Western New York since 1975, offering checking, savings, personal loans, and home loans.
description_long:
Kaleida Health Federal Credit Union (KHFCU) was founded in 1975 as a not-for-profit, member-owned financial institution serving healthcare workers in Niagara and Erie County. With over 3,400 members, KHFCU operates as a federally regulated credit union that combines bank-like services with member-centric advantages typical of the credit union model.

KHFCU offers deposit accounts (checking and savings), personal loans, home equity loans, and home loans with competitive rates. Members can access shared branching and ATM networks. The credit union provides a Member Advantage Program and maintains an online banking portal for account management. Loan products include home loans, home equity solutions, and personal loans tailored to individual member needs.

What distinguishes KHFCU is its low-income credit union designation granted in 2019, which enables the institution to serve healthcare professionals with limited or no credit history. The credit union explicitly markets its willingness to provide "second chance" financing when traditional banks deny applications. This positioning reflects a stated mission to support healthcare professionals throughout Western New York with flexible lending and financial education, regardless of prior credit challenges. Fee structures are reported to be lower than most banks, sometimes with no fees at all.

As a credit union, KHFCU returns earnings to members through higher interest rates on savings accounts and lower rates on loans. However, membership is restricted to healthcare professionals and their families employed within the service area—this is not a banking option for general consumers. The institution's scale (3,400 members) and geographic limitation to Western New York also means fewer physical locations and services compared to larger national banks.
services: Second-chance lending for members with limited/no credit
pros: Low-income credit union designation allows lending to members with bad credit, no credit, or limited credit history
Member-owned structure means earnings are returned as higher savings rates and lower loan rates
Founded in 1975 with established reputation and federal regulation
Lower fee structures than most banks, sometimes with no fees
Serves healthcare professionals and their families in Niagara and Erie County
Offers flexible personal and home loan solutions tailored to member needs
Access to shared branching and ATM networks beyond their own branches
cons: Membership restricted to healthcare professionals and their families in service area—not available to general public
Geographic limitation to Niagara and Erie County in Western New York reduces accessibility
Limited to 3,400+ members, suggesting fewer physical branches and resources compared to larger financial institutions
Website lacks specific rate information, APR details, or loan terms—requires direct contact for pricing
No information on mobile banking app capabilities or digital service sophistication
best_for: Healthcare professionals in Western New York with credit challenges seeking second-chance financing
Kaleida Health employees looking for member-exclusive financial services and competitive rates
Healthcare workers seeking lower fees and personalized loan solutions from a not-for-profit institution
Credit union members prioritizing member ownership and earnings return over bank shareholder profits
diagnosis: KHFCU is best for healthcare professionals in Western New York seeking flexible lending and lower fees through a member-owned credit union, particularly those with credit challenges. The main caveat is strict membership eligibility limited to healthcare workers in the service area—this institution is not an option for general consumers or those outside Niagara/Erie County.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
